name: Route Live with the River from Villel to Villaspesa (PR-TE 94)
name_original: Ruta Vivir con el Río desde Villel hasta Villaspesa (PR-TE 94)
region_intro: Hiking around Villel explores the varied terrain of the Teruel province in Aragon, Spain. The landscape features river valleys, such as the Ebrón River, with gorges and waterfalls, alongside pine forests and natural lagoons. These trails offer a mix of riverside paths and routes through more elevated, forested areas. Best hiking trails around Villel The most popular hiking route is Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain through the Amanaderos area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de Bezas and Balsa del Pinar – circular from Rubiales, a moderate 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This route explores a lagoon and pine pond area, offering a mix of natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Gorges of the Ebrón River – Calicanto Waterfall loop from Tormón, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through river gorges and past a waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Villel is defined by river valleys, gorges, waterfalls, and pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Villel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Villel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villel?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes available around Villel, offering a diverse range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of hiking trails in Villel?
A: The trails around Villel cater to various skill levels. You'll find 4 easy routes, 10 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners and experienced hikers alike.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Villel?
A: Yes, Villel offers several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974593688/amanaderos-de-riodeva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.3-mile (13.4 km) moderate trail that takes about 4 hours to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976361402/laguna-de-bezas-and-balsa-del-pinar-circular-from-rubiales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas and Balsa del Pinar – circular from Rubiales&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.3-mile (15.0 km) moderate path exploring a lagoon and pine pond area.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls near Villel?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its river valleys and gorges, featuring beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Ebrón River – Calicanto Waterfall loop from Tormón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.6-mile (9.0 km) trail that leads directly past the Calicanto Waterfall.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners in Villel?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These trails offer a gentle introduction to the varied terrain of the Teruel province.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking trails near Villel?
A: For those seeking longer adventures, Villel has options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981549377/route-live-with-the-river-from-villel-to-villaspesa-pr-te-94" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route Live with the River from Villel to Villaspesa (PR-TE 94)&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 15.3-mile (24.6 km) route that follows the river, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974625544/amanaderos-waterfalls-and-gr-10-trail-from-camarena-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 14.6-mile (23.4 km) hike.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking around Villel allows you to explore several natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Pinar&lt;/a&gt;, both lakes offering serene natural beauty. The area also features the unique geological formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;, a canyon worth exploring.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villel?
A: The hiking trails in Villel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys and gorges to pine forests, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic overlooks on the trails?
A: Yes, for panoramic views, consider hiking to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz (Peña Botadera) Lookout&lt;/a&gt;. This viewpoint offers stunning vistas of the surrounding landscape, characteristic of the Teruel province.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect while hiking in Villel?
A: The landscape around Villel is characterized by diverse natural features. You'll encounter river valleys, such as the Ebrón River, with impressive gorges and waterfalls. The trails also wind through extensive pine forests and past natural lagoons, offering a mix of riverside paths and elevated, forested areas.

Q: Is Villel a good destination for exploring natural monuments?
A: Yes, Villel is situated in a region rich in natural monuments. Beyond the hiking trails, you can explore features like the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt; canyon, the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, all contributing to the area's natural beauty.
tips: Very quiet and picturesque town a few kilometers from the city of Teruel. Its places of interest include the FuenSanta Sanctuary, the Barranco de los Trancos Route and the well-known Living with the River Route. A town that has a lot to offer and is worth visiting.

The path next to the Turia ravine is a very picturesque walk that connects the towns of Villel and Villaspesa. It is not difficult and if I recommend you do it in the spring or autumn months, the sun can be very hard in summer!

This modernist building was built between 1911 and 1912 by the Catalan architect Pablo Monguió, who worked extensively in the community of Teruel at the beginning of the 20th century. It is an interpretation of medieval religious architecture, fusing historicist references with the aesthetic language of the early 20th century. It is located a short distance from the city of Teruel, about five kilometers.
